text: We investigate the strengths and limitations of neural machine translation (NMT) approaches to statistical translation and examine the performance of two types of neural translation models: (1) the state-of-the-art RNN Encoder-Decoder, and (2) a novel gated recursive convolutional neural network (grConv) approach. We compare their capabilities in translating various types of input sentences, with a focus on exploring the effects of increasing sentence length on the accuracy of the NMT models. Experimental results show that, although NMT models are highly proficient in translating short sentences and for well-known vocabularies, their performance deteriorates drastically as the input length of a sentence increases, and especially when encountering the lack of knowledge of some uncommon words. Experiments also show that our proposed grConv model can learn and extract meaningful grammatical structures from input data in an unsupervised manner.
explanation: The passage reads like a cohesive academic abstract with specific model names and plausible experimental claims, showing no obvious AI-style repetition or generic phrasing.
